articles.title: Native Americans Fought in the Revolution to Save Their Land — But Lost It Anyway
articles.summary: Did you know Native Americans fought on both sides of the American Revolution? The Haudenosaunee Confederacy, a group of six Native nations, tried to stay neutral at first. But both the British and the colonists pressured them to pick a side. The Oneida and Tuscarora helped the colonists, while the Mohawk, Onondaga, Cayuga, and Seneca sided with the British. At the Battle of Oriskany in 1777, Native soldiers fought each other for the first time in hundreds of years. After the war, settlers took their land anyway. This story shows the tough choices Native nations made to protect their homes.

articles.title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss French Open Due to Wrist Injury
articles.summary: Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old world number two, has to skip the French Open next month because of a wrist injury. He hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona and tests showed he needs to rest. Alcaraz won the French Open the last two years, so he won't get to defend his title. He also won the Australian Open this year, becoming the youngest man to win all four major tournaments. His rival Jannik Sinner, who just beat him in Monte Carlo, said he hopes Alcaraz recovers soon. Sinner now has a chance to win the French Open and complete his own career Grand Slam.

articles.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Inherited Deafness — Kids Can Hear Whispers!
articles.summary: Big news in medicine! The FDA has approved the first gene therapy for a type of inherited deafness. The treatment, called Otarmeni, helps kids and adults who are born deaf because of a problem with a gene called OTOF. Doctors use a harmless virus to deliver a working copy of the gene into the ear. In a trial, 16 out of 20 people could hear better within six months, and some could even hear whispers! The company says the treatment itself is free for patients in the U.S. This is a huge step forward for gene therapy and could help many more kids in the future.
